_comment: Canonical source for the 8+1 hard rules. Edit HERE, then run `npm run generate` to regenerate the rule sections in CLAUDE.md (worker style) and MADDU.md (brief style). The generated-artifacts-current gate fails if a brief drifts from this registry. Each field is an array of lines joined with newlines; each rule is an array of lines (continuation lines carry their own indentation).

worker.heading: ## The 8+1 hard rules (do not violate)
banner: > **⚠️ SCOPE — read this first.** These invariants govern **how Máddu itself
> is built** — Máddu's own orchestration code under `.maddu/` and `maddu/`
> (the CLI, the bridge server, the cockpit). **They do NOT constrain the
> product / host project you are building with Máddu.** Your application may
> use any provider SDK, hosted backend, database (SQLite, Postgres, hosted
> DB), OAuth/token storage, cron, or real publishing engine it needs —
> whatever the product requires is the product's call, governed by the
> **project's own** `CLAUDE.md` (the repo-root one), not by Máddu. Máddu
> orchestrates the work; it never dictates the product's architecture. So:
> "Máddu forbids token storage / SDKs / hosted backends" is **only** true of
> Máddu's framework layer — never a reason to cripple, mock, or stub the
> thing you're shipping. When in doubt, build the real product feature.
intro: rule is about the **Máddu framework layer**):
rules: **Files-only state.** No SQLite, no embedded DB, no hosted DB for *Máddu's* feature state.
**Append-only event spine.** The spine wins over any projection.
**No hosted backends** *(for Máddu)*. Máddu calls provider APIs from local subprocess workers; it ships no SaaS. (Your product may have any backend it wants.)
**No broad new dependencies** *(in Máddu)*. Máddu's own code is Node stdlib where possible. (Your product's `package.json` is yours.)
**No provider SDKs in *Máddu's* app code** — the bridge / cockpit / harness never import an SDK; only worker subprocesses own the API call. (Your product may import any SDK.)
**No token export** *(of Máddu's OAuth tokens)*. Máddu's tokens are device-bound at
`~/.config/maddu/auth/` (Linux/macOS) or `%APPDATA%\maddu\auth\` (Windows).
`maddu export` scrubs them; `maddu import` refuses to overwrite them.
**Three-layer brand boundary.** Framework shell brand / app brand /
content brand never mix. The cockpit's `tokens.css` is owned by Máddu and
must not be referenced from app or content code.
**Lane ownership.** No two agents may hold the same lane concurrently. Use
the mailbox bus (`.maddu/lanes/<lane>/mailbox.ndjson`) for cross-lane
handoffs, not shared mutation.
**Every auto-trigger crosses the gauntlet** (permanent since v0.19.0). No
spine/state/workspace-mutating command auto-fires without a `tier:'mutating'`
entry, an allowlist entry in `.maddu/config/triggers.json`, a respected
cooldown, and a `TRIGGER_FIRED` event carrying `triggered_by` provenance.

brief.heading: ## The 8+1 hard rules
banner: > **⚠️ SCOPE.** These rules govern **how Máddu itself is built** (Máddu's own
> code under `.maddu/` and `maddu/` — the CLI, bridge, cockpit). They are
> **NOT** constraints on the product / host project you are building *with*
> Máddu. Your application may use any SDK, hosted backend, database,
> OAuth/token storage, cron, or real publishing engine it needs — that's the
> project's call, governed by the repo-root `CLAUDE.md`, not by Máddu. Never
> stub, mock, or cripple a product feature because of a Máddu rule; build the
> real thing. `maddu doctor` only ever checks Máddu's own framework files.
intro: `maddu doctor` verifies all of these (against the **framework layer only**) on
every run. Full rationale in `maddu/docs/hard-rules.md`; the stable charter is
rules: **Files-only state** *(Máddu's)*. No SQLite/embedded/hosted DB for *Máddu's* feature state.
**Append-only event spine.** The spine wins over any projection.
**No hosted backends** *(Máddu has none)*. Máddu calls provider APIs from local subprocess workers.
**No broad new dependencies** *(in Máddu)*. Máddu's own code is Node stdlib where possible.
**No provider SDKs in *Máddu's* app code.** Only worker subprocesses own the API call. (Your product may import any SDK.)
**No token export** *(of Máddu's tokens)*. Máddu's OAuth tokens are device-bound. `maddu export` scrubs them.
**Three-layer brand boundary.** Framework shell brand / app brand / content brand never mix.
**Lane ownership.** No two agents may hold the same lane concurrently.
**Every auto-trigger crosses the gauntlet** (permanent since v0.19.0) — scope-lock, gates, allowlist, cooldown.
